commit.message:
scripts: rewrite build_pgo_lto as Python, improve benchmark ergonomics

build_pgo_lto.py replaces build_pgo_lto.sh:
- Always builds in a fresh temp directory, avoiding stale profdata reuse
- Accepts an optional git revision and exports sources via git archive
  rather than git-worktree
- Requires -o/--output so each benchmark binary gets an explicit name
- Leaves temporary build artifacts in /tmp for later inspection
- Auto-discovers llvm-profdata next to the selected compiler
- Removes the ad hoc benchmark examples from the script header

benchmark_compile_perf.py:
- Replace the fixed baseline/tested model with named repeated compiler
  specs and repeated runners
- Support runner-specific sampling knobs and passthrough arguments for
  hyperfine and callgrind
- Generalise console, JSON, and TSV output to arbitrary compiler sets
- Make --fail-fast stop the full invocation across runners
- Reject hyperfine passthroughs that would silently benchmark extra
  positional commands

Python scripts and docs:
- Reformat the scripts/ tree with ruff and fix the remaining decode.py
  lint issues
- Add scripts/format_lint_py.sh for Python formatting and linting
- Update README.md and AGENTS.md examples to the new benchmark CLI
  and helper naming

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Refactor web chat transcript and run state (#363)

* refactor(web): normalize chat transcript and run state

Summary:
- add shared web chat types and a local normalized transcript hook
- store messages by id/order and runs by run_id in the chat page
- derive evidence panel state from run-linked records and sync the
  web lockfile to the checked-in dependencies

Rationale:
- remove client_id and array-scan updates from streamed and
  action-required chat flows
- keep loaded sessions, trace selection, and source resolution tied
  to the latest run record
- keep the web workspace installable so lint and build validation can
  run against the committed dependency graph

Tests:
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm lint
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm build
- manual smoke via output/playwright-smoke/manual-smoke.js against a
  local mock API

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): restore repo lint and API test setup

Summary:
- fix repo-wide ruff violations in the legacy GUI module and API tests
- add the missing Weaviate lazy-import helper used by the legacy GUI
- install the API extra in CI and include python-multipart in the
  project API dependencies

Rationale:
- the PR test matrix was failing before pytest because repo-level lint
  issues in untouched Python files were red
- the Linux test job installs only `.[dev]`, so API tests would fail on
  FastAPI upload route imports once lint passed
- keeping these fixes separate from the web refactor makes the branch
  history reviewable while still unblocking green checks

Tests:
- .venv-ci/bin/ruff check . --exclude .venv-ci
- .venv-ci/bin/python -m pytest -q
- .venv-ci/bin/python main.py --cli --help

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(ci): skip action card test without Qt runtime

Summary:
- gate the action card widget test behind importorskip for the Qt
  modules and dependent view modules
- align the skip behavior with the rest of the Qt-focused test suite

Rationale:
- the Linux test matrix was failing during collection when PySide6 was
  importable but its runtime EGL libraries were unavailable
- the widget test should skip in that environment instead of crashing
  before the rest of the suite can run

Tests:
- .venv-ci/bin/ruff check . --exclude .venv-ci
- .venv-ci/bin/python -m pytest -q
- .venv-ci/bin/python main.py --cli --help

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(codex): harden PR lookup and auto review

Summary:
- fix runtime ticket delivery to resolve PR URLs with a valid
  branch-scoped gh pr view invocation
- extend app-server coverage for both PR create and existing-PR edit
  delivery paths
- add an explicit Claude review prompt to the workflow_run review job
  and guard it in repository tests

Rationale:
- GitHub CLI rejects gh pr view with --repo alone, which blocked
  runtime delivery after PR creation
- the external review workflow should run automatically after CI
  instead of depending on @claude mentions
- repository guard tests should lock both behaviors in to prevent
  regressions

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(workflow): add shared checkout repair flow (#28)

Summary:
- add a checkout repair script and npm alias that rebuild the shared
  repo from .bootstrap/project.json
- preserve the previous checkout as a timestamped backup and restore it
  automatically if the reclone fails
- document the maintenance flow and add regression tests for the repair
  scenarios

Rationale:
- the shared /home/helionaut/src/projects/run-pace-calculator checkout
  had been left on a conflicted HEL-16 branch and was unsafe for new
  work
- a repo-local repair path is safer and repeatable than manual cleanup
  because it recreates the checkout from current remote state and keeps
  the old tree for investigation

Tests:
- node --test tests/repair-shared-checkout.test.js
- npm run check
- ./scripts/repair-shared-checkout.sh

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(websocket): add recker integration coverage

Summary:
- add recker as a dev dependency for websocket integration testing
- cover a Raffel custom websocket server with a Recker client
- verify a welcome frame plus 10 request and response exchanges

Rationale:
- this proves Raffel interoperates with Recker over a real websocket session
- it also gives us a regression test for multi-message custom protocol flows

Tests:
- pnpm vitest run -c vitest.config.int.ts test/adapters/websocket-custom-protocol.int.test.ts
- pnpm typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(repo): harden delivery and review automation

Summary:
- move runtime ticket delivery onto verify-gated PR publishing and
  template-faithful PR content
- extract a pure status request handler and rewrite status server tests
  to avoid binding localhost sockets in sandboxes
- update the GitHub review workflow for the current AWS region and
  Claude model routing

Rationale:
- successful ticket completion must stop before commit, push, PR, and
  Linear comment when repo verification fails
- status observability tests need to pass in restricted environments
  where loopback listeners are blocked
- the review workflow needs current cloud and model configuration to
  stay non-interactive and reliable

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(audit): support MCP logging in async paths

Summary:
- avoid calling `asyncio.run(...)` when integration audit logging is triggered inside an active event loop
- schedule integration audit writes on the running loop and cover the async MCP connect path with a regression test

Rationale:
- MCP connect and disconnect run from async app paths, so the previous sync helper could drop audit events in production
- keep the fail-open audit helper safe in both sync and async callers

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/audit/runtime.py backend/tests/test_mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_mcp_manager.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(websocket): reject invalid connections during handshake

Summary:
- move websocket filter and auth checks into handshake validation
- preserve auth seed from the upgrade request for accepted connections
- update integration tests to assert 401 and 403 handshake failures

Rationale:
- invalid websocket requests should fail before the upgrade completes
- this avoids transient opens followed by close events in browser clients

Tests:
- pnpm vitest run -c vitest.config.int.ts test/adapters/websocket.int.test.ts
- pnpm typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): own ticket delivery at runtime

Summary:
- add a complete_ticket_delivery tool that commits, pushes, creates or
  updates the PR, and posts the final Linear completion comment
- pass issue delivery metadata through AgentRunner and use structured
  delivery results as the primary success signal
- update the workflow prompt and repository guards to require the
  runtime-owned completion path

Rationale:
- prompt-only PR and comment instructions let Codex finish work without
  actually publishing the branch or including a PR URL in the handoff
- moving the delivery sequence into runtime code makes completion
  deterministic and lets the runner fail before posting an invalid
  completion comment

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(audit): trace MCP runtime lifecycle

Summary:
- add fail-open integration runtime audit helpers for external lifecycle events
- record MCP server connect, disconnect, auth-required, and failure states in structured runtime audit events
- extend MCP manager tests and runtime reliability docs to cover the new integration observability surface

Rationale:
- close another remaining runtime observability gap around external integration lifecycle behavior
- make MCP connection/auth failures easier to debug without turning audit issues into runtime failures

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/audit/runtime.py backend/src/tools/mcp_manager.py backend/tests/test_mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): inline distance controls and rebalance split rows (#27)

Summary:
- replace the distance increment set with -100m, -200m, +100m,
  and +200m in the distance header row
- add heading inset spacing and rebalance split-row metric layout
  for a more even single-line summary
- update calculator, DOM, and CSS regression tests plus refresh PR
  draft metadata for HEL-35

Rationale:
- reclaim vertical space in the distance card without losing quick
  distance adjustment controls
- improve scanability by pulling section headings away from the card
  edge and centering the split metrics more evenly
- allow negative adjustments while preventing invalid non-positive
  distances from being applied

Tests:
- npm run check
- built preview reviewed with desktop and iPhone 13 screenshots

commit.message:
fix(audit): bind strategist runtime context

Summary:
- bind a synthetic runtime session id before strategist scheduler agent runs so wrapped strategist tools can emit audit events
- add a regression test that exercises a wrapped strategist tool through run_strategist_tick and asserts the tool audit event is written

- make the strategist tool audit coverage claimed by this PR real in the scheduler path

commit.message:
test(saas_client): add transport/retry/unhappy-path coverage (issue #34) (#35)

Adds 11 new scenarios covering branches not exercised by prior tests:
- Transport error paths for event flush, heartbeat, config pull, config ack
- Register non-retriable (401) and retriable (503) HTTP error statuses
- Event flush backoff suppression: second flush blocked until window expires
- Flush returns 0 when circuit breaker is open (before half-open window)
- Config pull non-retriable 403 does not apply bundle
- Config ack transport error does not prevent bundle from being applied
- queue_event with non-table argument returns validation error
- Heartbeat triggers periodic config pull when poll interval has elapsed

534 unit + 65 integration tests passing, luacheck clean.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore(workflow): harden PR publish guidance

Summary:
- rewrite workspace origins after local clone so workspace git pushes target
  the source repository's GitHub remote
- keep WORKFLOW.md focused on ticket delivery policy and move the exact
  non-interactive PR publish procedure into the push skill
- extend repository guard tests and operations notes to lock in the push
  workflow and workspace remote behavior

Rationale:
- agent workspaces need a usable GitHub origin to push branches and open
  pull requests reliably
- de-duplicating publish instructions reduces drift between the workflow
  prompt and the reusable push skill

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
  test/repository/workflow.test.ts
- npx prettier --check WORKFLOW.md .codex/skills/push/SKILL.md
  docs/operations.md test/repository/tooling.test.ts
  test/repository/workflow.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(audit): add transport-agnostic tool execution logs

Summary:
- add a shared audited tool wrapper that records tool_call, tool_result, and tool_failed events for real executions
- wire the audit wrapper into primary agent and specialist tool composition while removing duplicate websocket-side tool event writes
- add regression coverage for tool execution audit behavior and update the Runtime Reliability workstream checklist

Rationale:
- extend runtime observability beyond chat, scheduler, and provider fallback into actual tool execution paths
- keep tool audit behavior consistent across transports instead of depending on websocket-only streaming hooks

Tests:
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v

Summary:

  and index picker with guarded prop sync plus async fetch updates
- switch the evidence source tooltip trigger to the Base UI
  render prop API

  usage that surfaced once the original build blocker was removed

Rationale:
- clears the current chat frontend lint violations without changing
  the user flows those effects were coordinating
- preserves the copy-button tooltip behavior using the tooltip API
  this repo actually exposes
- keeps the fix narrowly scoped to the files needed for lint/build
  to pass on this branch

Summary:
- propagate tracked llm runtime request ids through chat, websocket, and strategist agent runs
- suppress agent-model runtime audit events after the caller has already timed out the request
- add a regression test covering the late-success-after-timeout case in llm runtime

Rationale:
- keep provider runtime observability consistent with the existing timeout-safe direct completion path
- address the PR review note about misleading late success and fallback events after agent timeouts

Tests:

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill redundant rejoin hash reference

Summary:
- replace the unreleased redundant rejoin changelog placeholder with the
  real short hash

Rationale:
- the repo policy requires release-note-worthy changes to carry their
  short commit hash once the implementation commit exists

Validation:
- not run (docs-only hash follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(redundant): warm restored links before full rejoin

Summary:
- track explicit redundant-port carrier restore/loss state in the
  redundant link adapter
- drain reopened ports and keep them out of the required reply set for
  one warm-up transaction before full rejoin
- add a deterministic redundant bus regression covering primary loss,
  restore, and immediate cyclic traffic

Rationale:
- the restored primary leg was being trusted immediately after reopen,
  which let late or stale replies stall the first post-reconnect cycle
- the bus needed a deterministic regression at the redundant link layer
  because the current simulator cannot model a single late rejoin leg

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs --warnings-as-errors
- mix format --check-formatted lib/ethercat/bus/link/redundant.ex test/ethercat/bus_test.exs CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(audit): expand provider runtime visibility

Summary:

commit.message:
InteractiveUtils: In `edit(m::Module)`, throw a more informative error when `pathof(m)` is nothing (#61303)

🤖 Discovered via automated Codex audit.

Before this PR, `edit(m::Module)` will throw a relatively unhelpful
error if `pathof(m)` is nothing:

```julia
julia> module MyModule end

julia> InteractiveUtils.edit(MyModule)
ERROR: object is not callable
Stacktrace:
```

After this PR, it throws a more useful error.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): pin npm registry to public npmjs

Summary:
- add a repo-level npm registry config for the public npmjs registry
- regenerate the lockfile so resolved package URLs no longer point at a
  private Nexus host
- add a repository guard that prevents private registry settings from leaking
  back into tracked npm inputs

Rationale:
- make GitHub Actions installs deterministic and independent of local user npm
  configuration
- prevent future CI failures caused by lockfiles generated against private
  workstation registries

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(review): sync blocking GitHub review feedback to Linear

Summary:
- add a github-review workflow that runs after successful make-all PR runs
- mirror blocking GitHub review findings into Linear and move issues back to
  Rework through a dedicated sync script
- require Linear issue identifiers in PR bodies and include recent Linear
  comments in the agent prompt for rework runs

Rationale:
- make external GitHub review part of the normal PR flow without giving GitHub
  authority over work state
- keep Linear as the single source of truth for rework dispatch while still
  surfacing reviewer feedback back to Symphony
- enforce a stable PR-to-Linear mapping so review results can be synced
  deterministically

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Implement a distinct FT2 protocol path in the decoder stack and align\nits timing with the confirmed FT2 framing used by Decodium.\n\nCo-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add a compact workout split builder below the calculator (#22)

* feat(ui): add a compact workout split builder

Summary:
- add split builder state and UI below the calculator with add,
  save, select, copy, and delete actions
- capture the current calculator values into compact split rows that
  show distance, pace, and time together
- tighten headings, spacing, and mobile layout to fit the new split
  workflow without horizontal overflow
- cover the new DOM flow with add/select/save/duplicate/delete tests

Rationale:
- lets runners turn the live calculator into a readable workout plan
  instead of re-entering each segment elsewhere
- preserves the current calculator editing model by saving stable
  state snapshots and only switching to save mode after real edits
- keeps the new planning UI compact enough for common mobile and
  laptop viewports

Tests:
- npm run check
- built-preview visual verification on desktop and mobile via a
  temporary Puppeteer run against dist

Summary:
- add the workout split builder to the shipped-feature list in README
- replace the stale PR draft and title metadata with HEL-30 details
- record the desktop and mobile preview notes used for this UI pass

Rationale:
- keeps the branch documentation aligned with the published behavior
- lets the repo PR scripts create accurate metadata for the current issue
- preserves the visual verification notes alongside the branch history

  like a compact workout editor rather than a card stack
- keeps the mobile experience dense while still exposing all three
  runner-facing metrics and the inline row actions
- makes the visual evidence and PR metadata reflect the shipped UI

which further reduces UI disruption during large history restores.

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): batch decode history replay

Replay decode history in decoder-specific batches instead of feeding every
message through the single-message path.

This reduces per-message array churn and UI scheduling during large history
loads while keeping the existing live decode behavior unchanged.

commit.message:
test(coverage): targeted unit tests for decision_api and saas_client (Issue #30) (#31)

Add 29 new scenarios across decision_api and saas_client to raise coverage:

decision_api.lua:
- Jitter hash fallbacks (crc32_short, hmac_sha256, sha1_bin unavailable)
- Provider detection (anthropic, openai_compatible, gemini, mistral, nil)
- ip_tor normalization via normalize_boolish (yes/no/1/0)
- Bundle descriptor hint suppresses user_agent when needs_user_agent=false
- access_handler 503 paths (rule engine missing, no bundle loaded)
- LLM rejection JSON body for tpm_exceeded and tpd_exceeded
- Retry-after gt_3600 bucket metric
- log_handler upstream error queues saas event, skips 429
- debug_session_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 403 (wrong secret), 204 (valid)
- debug_logout_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 204 (POST)

saas_client.lua:
- flush_events/pull_config guard when not initialized
- Edge token CR/LF injection uses empty bearer auth
- Event coalescing: same signature increments counter, summary on flush
- Subject ID hashing removes raw field, sets subject_id_hash
- Non-retriable 401 on event flush counts as error
- Bundle load rejection ack as rejected
- Half-open circuit failure reopens circuit
- Heartbeat JSON string body extract_payload branch
- Timer premature=true callback returns without HTTP request

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[feat](trx-client): add configurable decode history retention

Add a default decode history retention setting in minutes with per-rig overrides, resolve the active rig retention in the HTTP frontend runtime, and apply that retention consistently across backend decode history buffers and frontend decoder views. This removes fixed APRS, HF APRS, AIS, VDES, FT8, and WSPR browser-side history caps in favor of time-based pruning, and includes the pending longest-QSO card style reset.

streaming.lua, and bundle_loader.lua to push non-generated coverage
above the 82.28% baseline:

- utils: now() no-ngx, safe_require, to_hex, constant_time_equals,
  encode_base64 no-ngx, decode_base64 fallback, base64url_decode edge
  cases, sha256 nil guard, json additional branches, get_json chain
- streaming: validate_config type errors, is_streaming nil/non-table,
  body_filter truncated/buffer paths, include_partial_usage=false
- bundle_loader: ua descriptor hint, sha1-fallback HMAC, hmac
  unavailable, md5/pure hash fallbacks, no-timer init, file_not_found,
  saas_client audit event on apply

Closes #25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(lua-quality): remove duplicate local utils declaration in utils_spec

luacheck warned: variable utils re-defined on line 260, already declared
on line 3. Remove the redundant require at the Issue #25 section header.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(capture): render scaffolds without writing files

Summary:
- add capture rendering APIs that return generated driver and simulator
  source directly instead of always writing files
- support module-name strings, signal-name overrides, and formatted
  generated source for capture-backed scaffolds
- add focused tests for the render APIs and include ex_dna as a
  dev/test dependency

Rationale:
- make capture generation easier to reuse from tooling and tests
  without forcing filesystem writes as an intermediate step
- keep generated scaffold output more controllable and predictable
  for both direct generation and future editor or LLM-assisted flows

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs --warnings-as-errors

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(evals): add runtime reliability harness

Summary:
- add a deterministic runtime eval harness that exercises fallback-capable chat, onboarding, strategist, daily briefing, and shell timeout flows
- cover the new harness with dedicated tests and document how to run it from the backend testing guide
- update the Season 1 runtime reliability roadmap docs to reflect the shipped eval harness and the remaining open work

Rationale:
- give S1-B3 a repeatable reliability check that does not depend on live LLM providers
- keep fallback wiring and degraded behavior easy to verify after future runtime changes

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/evals/__init__.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py tests/test_daily_briefing.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_strategist.py tests/test_shell_tool.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario daily_briefing_fallback --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(observability): unify telemetry and structured logging

Summary:
- stabilize the runtime observability surface across master, bus,
  domain, DC, slave, and simulator paths
- add producer-path telemetry coverage for startup, configuration,
  activation, domain-cycle, and DC runtime events
- roll structured metadata through the remaining high-signal startup
  and process-data log paths

Rationale:
- keep telemetry machine-readable and bounded while making logs much
  easier to correlate and filter during failures and startup
- verify real producer paths so future refactors cannot silently drift
  from the documented telemetry contract

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/telemetry_test.exs test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_observability_test.exs
  test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
  test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs
  test/ethercat/slave_test.exs --warnings-as-errors

commit.message:
feat(llm): add fallback model for smolagents

Summary:
- add a fallback-capable LiteLLM model wrapper for smolagents-based agent paths
- restore timeout-safe audit tracking for direct completion fallback calls
- update the Season 1 runtime reliability docs to reflect the new agent-path coverage

Rationale:
- provider failure in the interactive agent runtime was still bypassing the shared fallback layer
- runtime progress docs should stay aligned with the reliability work that has actually landed

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/llm_runtime.py backend/src/agent/factory.py backend/src/agent/specialists.py backend/src/agent/strategist.py backend/src/agent/onboarding.py backend/tests/test_llm_runtime.py backend/tests/test_agent.py backend/tests/test_specialists.py backend/tests/test_delegation.py backend/tests/test_strategist.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_llm_runtime.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py tests/test_delegation.py tests/test_strategist.py tests/test_daily_briefing.py tests/test_timeouts.py tests/test_context_window.py
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(llm): audit fallback runtime behavior

Summary:
- add audit events for primary LLM success, primary failure, fallback success, and fallback failure inside the shared runtime helper
- extend runtime helper tests to assert the new observability behavior
- update the Season 1 runtime-reliability docs to reflect that fallback behavior is now visible after the fact

Rationale:
- configurable fallback is useful, but we still need a way to verify when Seraph degraded gracefully in practice
- lightweight audit events are the smallest step toward runtime observability without adding a full monitoring stack

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/llm_runtime.py backend/tests/test_llm_runtime.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_llm_runtime.py tests/test_audit_api.py tests/test_daily_briefing.py tests/test_timeouts.py tests/test_context_window.py
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(changelog): fill observability hash reference

Summary:
- replace the pending observability changelog hash with the committed
  short hash

Rationale:
- the repo guidance expects release-note-worthy unreleased entries to
  carry concrete short hashes once the commit exists

Validation:
- updated CHANGELOG.md reference to b57763a

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(observability): stabilize telemetry contracts

Summary:
- stabilize bus transaction stop metadata on success and error
- distinguish aggregate links from concrete endpoints in frame and
  link telemetry
- add DC runtime and slave startup retry events and reduce retry-loop
  log noise

Rationale:
- telemetry should be a stable, low-cardinality machine-readable
  contract across the repo
- logs should stay operator-focused instead of carrying repeated retry
  chatter that belongs in telemetry
- bounded reason and fault kinds are easier to consume in dashboards,
  alerts, and traces

Validation:
- mix format --check-formatted
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/telemetry_test.exs test/ethercat/bus_test.exs \
  --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/integration/support/expect_test.exs \
  --warnings-as-errors

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
